1→# Gossamer
     2→
     3→**Never lose the context behind your code again.**
     4→
     5→Gossamer is a desktop control panel for your Claude Code sessions. It indexes, searches, and backs up every AI session you've ever run — so the thinking, the decisions, the rabbit holes, the breakthroughs — all of it is preserved and searchable forever.
     6→
     7→And because Gossamer integrates with **[Entire.io](https://entire.io)**, your AI sessions are linked directly to your Git commits. Every line of code has a story. Now you can actually read it.
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## What it does
    12→
    13→- **Index** every Claude Code session automatically as you work
    14→- **Search** across all your sessions — find that conversation where you figured out the auth bug three months ago
    15→- **Back up** sessions so nothing is ever lost, even across machines
    16→- **Link sessions to commits** via Entire.io — click any commit and see the full AI conversation behind it
    17→- **Browse checkpoints** — step through the evolution of a session with diffs and context at every turn
    18→- **Resume sessions** — jump back into past conversations right where you left off
    19→
    20→---
    21→
    22→## Requirements
    23→
    24→- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org) v18+
    25→- [Entire CLI](https://entire.io) installed
    26→- Claude Code installed and configured
    27→
    28→> **Homebrew installs coming soon.** For now, clone and run locally.
    29→
    30→---
    31→
    32→## Getting started
    33→
    34→### 1. Install the Entire CLI and enable it in your repo
    35→
    36→Install the [Entire CLI](https://entire.io), then run the following inside any repo you want Gossamer to track:
    37→
    38→```bash
    39→entire enable
    40→```
    41→
    42→This hooks into your Git workflow so Gossamer can link AI sessions to commits.
    43→
    44→### 2. Clone Gossamer and start it
    45→
    46→```bash
    47→git clone https://github.com/your-org/gossamer.git
    48→cd gossamer
    49→npm run start
    50→```
    51→
    52→`npm run start` installs dependencies, builds, and launches both the backend server and the Electron desktop app together.
    53→
    54→### 3. Add your repo in the Repos tab
    55→
    56→Open Gossamer, go to the **Repos** tab, and add the local path to the repo you ran `entire enable` in. Gossamer will start indexing your sessions immediately.
    57→
    58→---
    59→
    60→## Architecture
    61→
    62→Gossamer has two components that run together:
    63→
    64→| Component | What it does |
    65→|-----------|-------------|
    66→| **Server** (`src/serve.ts`) | Node.js + Express + WebSocket server. Indexes Claude Code sessions from disk, syncs with Entire.io, and serves a real-time API on `localhost:3000`. |
    67→| **App** (`app/`) | Electron desktop app built with React + Mantine. Connects to the local server and renders your session history, search, checkpoints, and diffs. |
    68→
    69→Both start with `npm start`.
    70→
    71→---
    72→
    73→## How the Entire.io integration works
    74→
    75→Gossamer uses the Entire CLI to associate your Claude Code sessions with the Git commits they produced. When you make a commit while Claude Code is active, Entire captures the link between the session and the commit hash.
    76→
    77→From Gossamer you can:
    78→- See which session produced which commit
    79→- Open a commit and read the full AI conversation that led to it
    80→- Never wonder "why did we write it this way?" again
    81→
    82→Make sure the Entire CLI is installed and you're logged in before starting Gossamer.
    83→
    84→---
    85→
    86→## Your data, your machine
    87→
    88→Gossamer is built on a simple principle: your AI sessions are yours. No telemetry, no cloud sync, no third-party storage. Everything stays local by default.
    89→
    90→- **Search index and database** are stored in a single SQLite file per repo at `~/.gossamer/<YOUR_REPO>.db` — on your machine, readable with any SQLite tool, deletable whenever you want.
    91→- **Session history** is stored in your local Git repository. Push it to your own remote to back it up or share it across machines — on your terms, using infrastructure you already control.
    92→- **The server is local.** Gossamer's backend runs on `localhost` and never opens an external connection. The Electron app talks only to that local server.
    93→
    94→You decide what gets backed up, who has access, and when it gets deleted. Gossamer never touches your data without you.
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Development
    99→
   100→To work on the server and app separately:
   101→
   102→```bash
   103→# Server only (hot-reloads with tsc --watch)
   104→npm run serve
   105→
   106→# App only (Electron + Vite HMR)
   107→cd app && npm run dev
   108→```
   109→
   110→Database UI:
   111→
   112→```bash
   113→npm run db:studio
   114→```
   115→
   116→---
   117→
   118→## Coming soon
   119→
   120→- `brew install gossamer` — one-line install, no Node required
   121→- Team sharing — share session history with your whole org
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→Built for developers who care about the work behind the work.
   126→

- Prisma 7 ecosystem (`@prisma/client`, `@prisma/adapter-better-sqlite3`)
- SQLite support (`better-sqlite3`)
- Server: Express, WebSocket (ws), CORS
- Terminal: xterm, node-pty

- **Framework**: React 18 + Mantine UI

- **Router**: react-router-dom
- **Utilities**: diff2html (for diffs), xterm (terminal viewing)

---

### 2. **Project Structure**

**Top-level directories**:
```
/Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/
├── src/               # TypeScript source (backend)
├── app/               # Electron app (frontend)

└── README.md          # Project documentation
```

---

### 3. **Built Output (dist/ Directory)**

Contains compiled JavaScript from src/:
- `config.js` / `config.d.ts` — Configuration management
- `db.js` / `db.d.ts` — Database helpers (Prisma integration)
- `gitUtils.js` / `gitUtils.d.ts` — Git utilities
- `indexer.js` / `indexer.d.ts` — Session indexing logic (35KB)
- `search.js` / `search.d.ts` — Search functionality

---

### 6. **README.md Summary**

**Project**: Gossamer — "Never lose the context behind your code again."

**Core Features**:
- Index Claude Code sessions automatically
- Search across all sessions (find conversations from months ago)
- Backup sessions locally
- Link sessions to Git commits via Entire.io
- Browse session checkpoints with diffs
- Resume past sessions

**Architecture**:
- **Server** (`src/serve.ts`): Node.js + Express + WebSocket on `localhost:3000`
- **App** (`app/`): Electron desktop app (React + Mantine)

**Data Storage**:
- SQLite database per repo at `~/.gossamer/<REPO>.db`
- 100% local — no cloud, no telemetry
- Uses Git as backup transport (push to your own remote)

**Dev Environment**:
- Requires Node.js v18+, Entire CLI, Claude Code
- `npm run start` launches server + app together
- `npm run db:studio` for database UI

- `db.ts` — SQLite/Prisma database helpers

- `search.ts` — Search implementation
- `serve.ts` — Entry point (spawns server.ts)
- `server.ts` — Express HTTP + WebSocket API server

---

1. **Two-part architecture**: Backend (Node.js server) + Frontend (Electron desktop app)
2. **Database**: SQLite with Prisma 7 using better-sqlite3 adapter
3. **Real-time sync**: WebSocket broadcasts between local server and Electron app
4. **No external dependencies**: All data stays local, no cloud integrations
5. **Built-in terminal**: xterm + node-pty for embedded terminal sessions
6. **Active development**: Last commits March 20-22, 2025 (tabbed interface, subsessions)
7. **No release pipeline**: Manual builds only; suitable for direct git clone + npm install

content.text:
---

Here's the full process to make it available to others:

## What was created

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `app/package.json` | Added `electron-builder` + `build` config + `dist:mac` script |
| `.github/workflows/release.yml` | Builds the `.dmg` and publishes to GitHub Releases on any `v*` tag |
| `homebrew/gossamer.rb` | Cask template to copy into your tap repo |

## To publish a release

```bash
# 1. Install electron-builder
cd app && npm install

# 2. Bump the version in app/package.json if needed, then tag and push
git tag v1.0.0
git push origin v1.0.0
```

GitHub Actions will build `Gossamer-1.0.0-arm64.dmg` and `Gossamer-1.0.0-x64.dmg` and attach them to the release automatically.

## To make it `brew install`-able

You need a **tap repo** — a GitHub repo named `homebrew-gossamer` under your account.

```bash
# Create repo at github.com/nosman/homebrew-gossamer
# Then copy the cask file into it:

mkdir -p Casks
cp gossamer/homebrew/gossamer.rb Casks/gossamer.rb
```

After the release runs, get the SHA256 of each DMG:
```bash
shasum -a 256 Gossamer-1.0.0-arm64.dmg
shasum -a 256 Gossamer-1.0.0-x64.dmg
```

Update the `REPLACE_WITH_*` placeholders in `Casks/gossamer.rb` and push. Then users can install with:

```bash
brew tap nosman/gossamer
brew install --cask gossamer
```

## One caveat

The app is **unsigned** (no Apple Developer cert), so on first launch macOS will block it. Users need to: **System Settings → Privacy & Security → Open Anyway**, or you can right-click the `.app` → Open.

To remove this friction long-term you'd need an Apple Developer account ($99/yr) and add signing secrets to the GitHub Actions workflow.

message.content: why is  the installation process done from the app directory and not the top-level directory? the server-side code is in the top-level directory

content.text:
Good catch. The workflow only touches `app/` because the Cask only distributes the Electron `.app` bundle — and that bundle doesn't include or start the server. The server (`gossamer-serve`) is a separate process that users have to run themselves.

There are two ways to fix this:

**Option A — Bundle the server inside the Electron app.** The Electron main process spawns `gossamer-serve` on startup (pointing at a bundled `dist/` inside the `.app`). Users install one thing and it just works. This requires changes to `app/src/main/index.ts` and the `electron-builder` config to include the root `dist/` in the package.

**Option B — Also release the server as a separate artifact.** Add steps to the workflow to build the root package and attach a tarball to the same GitHub Release. Users install the Cask for the UI and separately install the server (via a Homebrew Formula, npm, or manually extracting the tarball).

Option A gives a much better user experience — one install, no separate server process to manage. Option B keeps the two pieces decoupled but puts more burden on the user.

Which would you prefer?

message.content: option A, bundle the server inside the app

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
  output   = "../prisma/generated/client"
}

datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
}

model InteractionOverview {
  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  sessionId String   @unique
  summary   String
  keywords  String   // JSON array as text: '["a","b"]'
  startedAt DateTime
  endedAt   DateTime
}

// ─── Entire CLI checkpoint tables ─────────────────────────────────────────────

model CheckpointMetadata {
  id               Int                          @id @default(autoincrement())
  cliVersion       String?
  checkpointId     String                       @unique
  strategy         String?
  branch           String?
  checkpointsCount Int                          @default(0)
  gitUserName      String?
  gitUserEmail     String?
  tokenUsageId     Int?                         @unique
  tokenUsage       TokenUsage?                  @relation("CheckpointMetadataTokenUsage", fields: [tokenUsageId], references: [id])
  sessions         SessionLink[]
  filesTouched     CheckpointMetadataFilePath[]
}

model SessionLink {
  id                   Int                 @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointMetadataId Int?
  checkpointMetadata   CheckpointMetadata? @relation(fields: [checkpointMetadataId], references: [id])
  metadata             String?
  transcript           String?
  context              String?
  contentHash          String?
  prompt               String?
  logEvents            LogEvent[]

  @@index([checkpointMetadataId])
}

model CheckpointMetadataFilePath {
  checkpointMetadataId Int
  filePathId           Int
  checkpointMetadata   CheckpointMetadata @relation(fields: [checkpointMetadataId], references: [id])
  filePath             FilePath           @relation("CheckpointMetadataFilePaths", fields: [filePathId], references: [id])

  @@id([checkpointMetadataId, filePathId])
}

model FilePath {
  id                     Int                                @id @default(autoincrement())
  path                   String                             @unique
  checkpointFiles        CheckpointMetadataFilePath[]        @relation("CheckpointMetadataFilePaths")
  checkpointSessionFiles CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePath[] @relation("CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePaths")
}

model TokenUsage {
  id                        Int                        @id @default(autoincrement())
  inputTokens               Int                        @default(0)
  cacheCreationTokens       Int                        @default(0)
  cacheReadTokens           Int                        @default(0)
  outputTokens              Int                        @default(0)
  apiCallCount              Int                        @default(0)
  checkpointMetadata        CheckpointMetadata?        @relation("CheckpointMetadataTokenUsage")
  checkpointSessionMetadata CheckpointSessionMetadata? @relation("CheckpointSessionMetadataTokenUsage")
}

model CheckpointSessionMetadata {
  id                  Int                                   @id @default(autoincrement())
  cliVersion          String?
  checkpointId        String
  sessionId           String
  strategy            String?
  createdAt           DateTime?
  branch              String?
  checkpointsCount    Int                                   @default(0)
  agent               String?
  turnId              String?
  tokenUsageId        Int?                                  @unique
  tokenUsage          TokenUsage?                           @relation("CheckpointSessionMetadataTokenUsage", fields: [tokenUsageId], references: [id])
  filesTouched        CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePath[]
  initialAttribution  InitialAttribution?
  summary             CheckpointSessionSummary?

  @@unique([checkpointId, sessionId])
  @@index([sessionId])
}

model CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePath {
  checkpointSessionMetadataId Int
  filePathId                  Int
  checkpointSessionMetadata   CheckpointSessionMetadata @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
  filePath                    FilePath                  @relation("CheckpointSessionMetadataFilePaths", fields: [filePathId], references: [id])

  @@id([checkpointSessionMetadataId, filePathId])
}

model InitialAttribution {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int                       @unique
  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionMetadata @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
  calculatedAt                  DateTime?
  agentLines                    Int                       @default(0)
  humanAdded                    Int                       @default(0)
  humanModified                 Int                       @default(0)
  humanRemoved                  Int                       @default(0)
  totalCommitted                Int                       @default(0)
  agentPercentage               Float                     @default(0)
}

model CheckpointSessionSummary {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int                       @unique
  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionMetadata @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
  intent                        String
  outcome                       String
  openItems           OpenItem[]
  frictionItems       FrictionItem[]
  repoLearnings       RepoLearning[]
  codeLearnings       CodeLearning[]
  workflowLearnings   WorkflowItem[]
}

model OpenItem {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionSummaryId    Int
  checkpointSessionSummary      CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionSummaryId], references: [id])
  text                          String
  status                        String                    @default("open") // "open" | "in_progress" | "complete" | "na"
  subSessionId                  String?

  @@unique([checkpointSessionSummaryId, text])
  @@index([checkpointSessionSummaryId])
}

model FrictionItem {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionSummaryId    Int
  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionSummaryId], references: [id])
  text                          String

  @@index([checkpointSessionSummaryId])
}

model RepoLearning {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int
  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
  text                          String

  @@index([checkpointSessionMetadataId])
}

model CodeLearning {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int
  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
  path                          String
  finding                       String

  @@index([checkpointSessionMetadataId])
}

model WorkflowItem {
  id                            Int                       @id @default(autoincrement())
  checkpointSessionMetadataId   Int
  checkpointSessionMetadata     CheckpointSessionSummary @relation(fields: [checkpointSessionMetadataId], references: [id])
  text                          String

  @@index([checkpointSessionMetadataId])
}


model CheckpointIdGitOidJoin {
  gitOid       String @id
  checkpointId String

  @@index([checkpointId])
}

model ArchivedSession {
  sessionId  String   @id
  archivedAt DateTime @default(now())
}

model SessionParent {
  childSessionId  String  @id
  parentSessionId String
  toolUseId       String?

  @@index([parentSessionId])
}

// ─── Full JSONL log tables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// One row per line in full.jsonl, with normalized sub-tables for content blocks,
// token usage, progress events, and system events.

// Tracks an in-progress session from a shadow branch (entire/<commit>-<hash>).
// Shadow sessions are indexed directly from git without a worktree.
// Once a session is committed to entire/checkpoints/v1 it also appears as a
// CheckpointSessionMetadata; the LogEvent uuid-dedup prevents double-indexing.
model ShadowSession {
  id            Int        @id @default(autoincrement())
  sessionId     String     @unique
  branch        String              // most recently active shadow branch
  seenBlobShas  String     @default("[]")  // JSON array of git blob SHAs already indexed
  prompt        String?
  cwd           String?
  gitBranch     String?             // the working branch inside the session
  createdAt     DateTime?
  logEvents     LogEvent[]
}

model LogEvent {
  id              Int             @id @default(autoincrement())
  uuid            String?         @unique  // present on most event types
  messageId       String?         @unique  // used by file-history-snapshot instead of uuid
  sessionId       String?
  parentUuid      String?
  type            String          // user | assistant | progress | system | file-history-snapshot
  timestamp       DateTime?
  cwd             String?
  gitBranch       String?
  version         String?
  slug            String?
  isSidechain     Boolean?
  userType        String?
  toolUseId       String?
  parentToolUseId String?
  requestId       String?         // present on assistant events
  data            String          // full raw JSON of the event line
  sessionLinkId   Int?
  sessionLink     SessionLink?    @relation(fields: [sessionLinkId], references: [id])
  shadowSessionId Int?
  shadowSession   ShadowSession?  @relation(fields: [shadowSessionId], references: [id])
  contents        LogContent[]
  usage           LogUsage?
  hookProgress    LogHookProgress?
  systemData      LogSystemEvent?

  @@index([sessionId])
  @@index([sessionId, timestamp])
  @@index([sessionId, id])
  @@index([type])
  @@index([sessionLinkId])
  @@index([shadowSessionId])
}

// One row per content block within a user or assistant message.
model LogContent {
  id                Int       @id @default(autoincrement())
  logEventId        Int
  logEvent          LogEvent  @relation(fields: [logEventId], references: [id])
  role              String    // user | assistant
  contentIndex      Int       // 0-based position in the content array
  contentType       String    // text | thinking | tool_use | tool_result
  text              String?   // type=text
  thinking          String?   // type=thinking
  toolUseId         String?   // type=tool_use (id field) or type=tool_result (tool_use_id field)
  toolName          String?   // type=tool_use
  toolInput         String?   // type=tool_use, JSON text of input object
  toolResultContent String?   // type=tool_result, stringified content
  isError           Boolean?  // type=tool_result

  @@index([logEventId])
  @@index([toolUseId])
}

// Token usage summary for assistant messages.
model LogUsage {
  id                       Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  logEventId               Int      @unique
  logEvent                 LogEvent @relation(fields: [logEventId], references: [id])
  model                    String?
  stopReason               String?
  inputTokens              Int      @default(0)
  cacheCreationInputTokens Int      @default(0)
  cacheReadInputTokens     Int      @default(0)
  outputTokens             Int      @default(0)
}

// The `data` sub-object present on progress events (type=hook_progress).
model LogHookProgress {
  id         Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  logEventId Int      @unique
  logEvent   LogEvent @relation(fields: [logEventId], references: [id])
  type       String?  // e.g. hook_progress
  hookEvent  String?  // e.g. PreToolUse, PostToolUse
  hookName   String?  // e.g. PreToolUse:Read
  command    String?  // shell command that was run
}

// Extra fields for system events.
model LogSystemEvent {
  id                    Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  logEventId            Int      @unique
  logEvent              LogEvent @relation(fields: [logEventId], references: [id])
  subtype               String?  // e.g. stop_hook_summary, turn_duration
  hookCount             Int?
  stopReason            String?
  preventedContinuation Boolean?
  level                 String?
  durationMs            Int?     // present on turn_duration subtype
}

content.text:
There are three non-obvious challenges here that affect the design:

1. **`server.ts` uses `import.meta.url` to find `PROJECT_ROOT`** — the `pushSchema` call resolves the Prisma binary relative to the server file's location, so the bundled layout must preserve this structure
2. **`utilityProcess.fork()` requires a CJS entry point** — the server is ESM, needs a CJS wrapper
3. **`prisma` is in `devDependencies`** — needed at runtime for `pushSchema`, must be moved to `dependencies`

Here's the plan:
- Create `app/resources/server-launcher.cjs` — CJS wrapper that `import()`s the ESM server
- Copy server's `dist/` + `node_modules/` + `prisma/` into `Contents/Resources/server/` via `extraResources` (preserving the path structure `import.meta.url` relies on)
- In production, `app/src/main/index.ts` starts the server as a `utilityProcess`
- CI rebuilds native modules against Electron's ABI before packaging

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  build-macos:
    runs-on: macos-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      # Build the server (TypeScript → dist/)
      - name: Install root dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build server
        run: npm run build

      # Install app deps so @electron/rebuild can find the Electron version
      - name: Install app dependencies
        working-directory: app
        run: npm ci

      # Recompile native modules (better-sqlite3, node-pty) against Electron's Node.js ABI
      - name: Rebuild native modules for Electron
        working-directory: app
        run: npx @electron/rebuild --module-dir ../ --only better-sqlite3,node-pty

      # Remove devDependencies from the root node_modules that get bundled into the app
      - name: Prune root devDependencies
        run: npm prune --production

      # Build the Electron app and package it as a DMG
      - name: Package Electron app (macOS)
        working-directory: app
        env:
          CSC_IDENTITY_AUTO_DISCOVERY: false
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: npm run dist:mac

      - name: Upload to GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          files: |
            app/release/**/*.dmg
            app/release/**/*.zip
          generate_release_notes: true
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

content.text:
Pushed. Here's a summary of how it all fits together at runtime:

```
Gossamer.app/
└── Contents/Resources/
    ├── app.asar          ← Electron renderer + main process
    └── server/
        ├── server-launcher.cjs   ← utilityProcess.fork() target
        ├── package.json          ← "type":"module" so Node treats dist/*.js as ESM
        ├── dist/serve.js         ← server entry (ESM, uses import.meta.url)
        ├── node_modules/         ← all production deps including prisma CLI
        └── prisma/
            ├── schema.prisma     ← needed by `prisma db push` on repo onboard
            └── generated/        ← Prisma client (used by dist/db.js)
```

**Key design decisions:**
- `server-launcher.cjs` bridges `utilityProcess.fork()` (CJS-only) → ESM server via `import()`
- `package.json` is bundled alongside so Node.js knows `dist/*.js` files are ESM
- `prisma` moved to production deps so `npm prune --production` keeps it (it's called at runtime by `pushSchema`)
- Native modules (`better-sqlite3`, `node-pty`) get rebuilt against Electron's ABI by `@electron/rebuild` in CI before packaging
- Dev workflow is unchanged — `ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL` presence gates whether the main process starts the server

message.content: Does it still make sense to have the app and server code in separate folders?

content.text:
Not really, not anymore. The separation made sense when they were genuinely independent programs — you'd run `gossamer-serve` in a terminal and connect the Electron app to it. But now that the server is bundled inside the app, the structure works against you:

- Two `package.json` files, two `node_modules`, two build steps to coordinate
- The `extraResources` config has to reach out with `../` paths to grab the server's dist and node_modules
- The CI workflow has to orchestrate root builds and app builds separately
- The root directory is now effectively just a build artifact (`dist/`) that gets packaged into the Electron app

**What a consolidated layout would look like:**

```
gossamer/
├── src/
│   ├── main/          ← Electron main process (currently app/src/main/)
│   ├── preload/       ← (currently app/src/preload/)
│   ├── renderer/      ← React frontend (currently app/src/renderer/)
│   └── server/        ← Node.js server (currently root src/)
├── prisma/
├── package.json       ← one package.json, one node_modules
└── electron.vite.config.ts
```

The server code would build alongside the main/preload/renderer targets in `electron-vite`, and `extraResources` would point to a local path instead of `../`. The `gossamer-serve` standalone CLI bin entry could still live in `package.json` if you want to support headless use.

**The main caveat**: it's a meaningful refactor — moving files, updating every import path, merging the two `package.json` files. Worth doing if you're actively developing this, less worth it if you just want a working Homebrew release.

content.text:
Done. To publish a release:

1. **Tag and push** from the gossamer repo — the GitHub Action will build the DMGs and attach them to the release:
   ```bash
   git tag v1.0.0 && git push origin v1.0.0
   ```

2. **Get the SHA256s** of the published DMGs (shown in the release assets), then update `Casks/gossamer.rb` in `homebrew-gossamer` with the real hashes and bump the version.

3. Users install with:
   ```bash
   brew tap nosman/gossamer
   brew install --cask gossamer
   ```
